fix: optimistic toggle for game mode switch

Toggle now updates UI instantly without waiting for server roundtrip.
Server confirmation still reconciles state for all players.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace BufferedStream+ArrayPool with zero-copy mmap PCAP reader

MmapPcapReader uses MemoryMappedFile for zero-copy I/O:
- Packet data points directly into mmap'd pages (no allocations, no copies)
- madvise(MADV_SEQUENTIAL) enables aggressive kernel read-ahead
- Segmented Memory<byte> views handle files >2GB (1GB segments)
- UnmanagedMemoryManager bridges unsafe pointers to Memory<byte>

TimestampMergedReplayer simplified:
- Removed ArrayPool lifecycle tracking (_previousRented, ReturnRented)
- Mmap data is valid for replayer lifetime — no buffer management needed
- FeedHandler.EnqueueCopy no longer needs deep copy

Performance (52M packets, 15.7M orders, 3,639 books):
- Before: ~29s (BufferedStream + ArrayPool)
- After:  ~25s cold cache, ~20s warm cache (2.6M pkts/s)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: chat mobile - threads hidden on phone + send error feedback

Root cause: CSS .chat-thread { display: none } on mobile was permanent
in ProjectThreadsTab because it never set chat-layout--mobile-thread class.
Result: messages panel completely invisible on mobile in project Wątki tab.

Fixes:
- ProjectThreadsTab: add mobileView state + ChevronLeft back button
  (same pattern as ChatPage) — thread list / messages panel toggle on mobile
- CSS: add .chat-layout--embedded.chat-layout--mobile-thread overrides
  so embedded layout respects mobile-thread/mobile-list states
- useSendThreadMessage: add onError toast + console.error for send failures
  so user sees what went wrong instead of silent failure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix print engine offset for non-zero viewBox origin elements

Forge-exported elements have a tight viewBox with non-zero x/y origin
(e.g. '85 196 930 494'). The print engine was computing the inner
transform offset as -w/2, -h/2 without subtracting the origin, causing
elements to shift down/right in print vs workspace.

Fix: offX = -vb[0] - vb[2]/2, offY = -vb[1] - vb[3]/2

Native elements (0 0 w h origin) are unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Expand features: 12 tunnel cmds, TUI modals, BLE auto-detect

New tunnel commands (JS-verified):
- display_scroll(), display_set_brightness(), display_rotate()
- sound_set_volume(), sound_get_volume()
- motor_pwm(), motor_go_direction_to_position()
- move_pair_distance(), button_pressed()
- battery_voltage(), battery_current(), hub_get_hardware_id()

New Hub convenience methods:
- display_scroll/set_brightness/rotate, sound_set/get_volume
- motor_pwm, motor_go_direction_to_position, motor_absolute_position
- battery_voltage/current

TUI enhancements (1246 lines):
- HubLightModal: color picker for hub status LED (11 colors)
- ReplModal: interactive MicroPython REPL with eval_python
- BatteryModal: voltage/current/charger details
- New keybindings: [l]ight, [r]epl, [b]attery

BLE transport improvements:
- Auto-detect Atlantis vs LWP3 GATT services
- Dynamic TX/RX UUID selection
- MTU logging

Stats: 112 Hub methods, 63 tunnel commands, 8714 lines total

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: time-attack game mode (2-minute timed matches)

- New GameMode enum: CLASSIC (default) and TIME_ATTACK
- Server-authoritative 120-second timer, decremented each tick
- Highest score wins when time expires; ties = all win
- Host toggles mode in lobby with a switch control
- Guests see selected mode before game starts
- Countdown timer displayed in game HUD, pulses red at <30s
- Classic mode completely unaffected (no timer)
- New socket events: timeUpdate, gameModeChanged, setGameMode
- Added status-danger color token for timer warning

commit.message:
Fix TTS playback and keyboard scroll-to-bottom

TTS fix:
- Move Audio.setAudioModeAsync into playTts function (called
  right before each playback attempt). Setting it once on mount
  was unreliable — iOS audio session needs to be configured
  when actually playing, not at app startup.

Keyboard scroll fix:
- Add Keyboard.addListener('keyboardDidShow') that scrolls
  FlatList to bottom when keyboard appears
- 150ms delay to let KeyboardAvoidingView finish its layout
  shift before scrolling

commit.message:
Fix inspector scroll crash and input positioning

Inspector Panel:
- Use Modal animationType='slide' instead of manual Animated
- Separate tap-to-dismiss area (top 15%) from sheet content
- Sheet content no longer wrapped in TouchableOpacity (was
  intercepting scroll gestures and causing dismiss on scroll)
- ScrollView now has flex:1 with proper contentContainer padding
- Panel scrolls freely without dismissing

Chat Input:
- Restore SafeAreaView edges=['bottom'] around input (prevents
  input from being hidden behind home indicator)
- SafeAreaView sits inside KeyboardAvoidingView so keyboard
  push-up still works correctly
- Minimal padding on input wrapper (6px)

commit.message:
fix: add image codec security limits and validation helpers (#404)

* fix(tasks): harden image raster codec against decoder bombs, buffer aliasing, and silent SVG/GIF re-encoding

Three HIGH-severity issues in the image raster codec shared by every Image*Task:

1. **Decoder bombs** — `decodeDataUri` had no pixel/byte budget on either the
   Node or browser path. Now enforces `MAX_DECODED_PIXELS = 100_000_000` via
   sharp's `limitInputPixels` (Node) and a post-`createImageBitmap` dimension
   check (browser). Adds a compressed-byte pre-filter:
   `MAX_INPUT_BYTES_NODE = 64 MiB`, `MAX_INPUT_BYTES_BROWSER = 8 MiB` (tighter
   because `createImageBitmap` decompresses before the JS side can measure).

2. **Buffer aliasing in the Node codec** — `decodeDataUri` returned
   `new Uint8ClampedArray(data.buffer, data.byteOffset, data.byteLength)` over
   a Node Buffer from `sharp().raw().toBuffer()`. Because Node Buffers up to
   ~4 KiB are sliced out of a shared pool, this view aliased pool memory and
   downstream pixel mutations could corrupt unrelated pooled allocations (a
   real hazard for small images like 1×1 PNGs). Now copies via
   `new Uint8ClampedArray(data)`, matching the existing pattern in
   `expandGrayAlphaToRgba`.

3. **SVG/GIF silently re-encoded as PNG + SSRF-latent fetch** —
   `normalizeMimeType` silently fell through to PNG for any unknown format, so
   `image/svg+xml` was rasterized (vector info lost) and `image/gif` lost
   animation frames with no signal to the caller. The new
   `normalizeOutputMimeType` throws on unsupported types. Additionally,
   `REJECTED_DECODE_MIME_TYPES` rejects `image/svg+xml`, `image/svg`,
   `image/gif`, and `image/apng` at decode time. The browser codec also now
   asserts `startsWith("data:")` before calling `fetch` — defense in depth
   against a future caller bypassing the `isDataUriImage` gate in
   `imageTaskTransport.ts` and turning it into SSRF (`http://`, `file://`).

Implementation notes:
- New `imageCodecLimits.ts` owns the security policy (constants + pure
  helpers) so both codec files share a single source of truth and the helpers
  can be unit-tested directly.
- Browser `decodeDataUri` wraps `createImageBitmap` in `try/finally` so
  `bmp.close()` runs even when the pixel-budget check rejects, avoiding a
  decompressed-bitmap leak on every oversized input.
- No changes to the `ImageRasterCodec` interface or `imageTaskTransport.ts`.
- Known limitations: APNG declared as `image/png` and animated WebP declared
  as `image/webp` cannot be distinguished by mime alone; true rejection would
  require post-decode metadata inspection.

Tests:
- New `ImageCodec.test.ts` with 22 tests covering every helper (pixel budget,
  byte budget, data-URI assertion, mime extraction, output normalization,
  rejected-type set) plus end-to-end codec tests for SSRF, SVG/GIF decode
  rejection, encode-time format rejection, happy-path PNG round-trip, and a
  direct check that the decoded `Uint8ClampedArray.buffer.byteLength` equals
  the view's `byteLength` (catches pool-aliasing regressions).
- Existing `ImageTask.test.ts` (41 tests) remains green.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PPrUZTgbxoQW72ri2F1Bex

* fix(tasks): redact data URI payload in assertIsDataUri errors; pre-check byte budget before Buffer.from

commit.message:
feat: add `client-id` input and deprecate `app-id` (#353)

GitHub now recommends using a GitHub App's Client ID for authentication.
This PR adds a first-class `client-id` input, keeps `app-id` available
for compatibility, and makes the migration path explicit in both runtime
behavior and documentation.

### Action inputs

- Adds a new `client-id` input
- Removes `required` from `app-id`
- Marks `app-id` as deprecated in `action.yml`

### Runtime behavior

- Updates input parsing to prefer `client-id`
- Falls back to `app-id` for existing workflows
- Adds a clear error when neither `client-id` nor `app-id` is provided

### Docs

- Updates the README to recommend `client-id`
- Switches usage examples to `client-id`
- Documents that `app-id` is deprecated and that `client-id` takes
precedence if both are set

### Regression coverage

- Adds a focused test proving a client-ID-shaped value works through the
new `client-id` input
- Adds coverage for the missing-ID validation path
- Updates snapshots to lock in the new metadata and runtime behavior

### Resulting usage

Users can migrate to the new input name directly:

```yaml
- uses: actions/create-github-app-token@v3
  with:
    client-id: ${{ vars.GITHUB_APP_CLIENT_ID }}
    private-key: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY }}
```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: parkerbxyz <17183625+parkerbxyz@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace SortedDictionary with inverted sorted List + swap-remove in BookSide

Benchmark validated: InvertedList+SwapRemove is 1.6x-13.4x faster than
SortedDictionary depending on dataset size, with 20% less memory.

Key changes:
- BookSide now uses List<(long Price, List<OrderBookEntry>)> sorted with
  best price at the END of the array for O(1) amortized operations
- Binary search for price level lookup O(log n)
- Swap-remove for O(1) order deletion within price levels
- PriceLevelIndex tracking on OrderBookEntry for swap-remove
- Validate() updated to check sort order and PriceLevelIndex consistency
- Added GetOrdersAtPrice() and LevelCount for direct access
- PriceLevels property iterates end→start (best→worst) for API compat
- Added BenchmarkDotNet project in benchmarks/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(czds): register SIGTERM handler before initial sync to prevent zombie containers

The _shutdown function was defined as a closure inside main() and only
registered AFTER run_catchup_cycle() completed. During the initial multi-hour
catchup cycle, SIGTERM had no registered handler — leading to zombie containers
that survived Docker rolling deploys indefinitely.

Fix:
- Extract _shutdown to module level (mirrors openintel_ingestor.py pattern)
- Register signal.signal(SIGTERM/SIGINT) at the very start of main(), before
  run_catchup_cycle(), so Docker's stop_grace_period actually works
- Reference global _scheduler_ref instead of the closed-over local 'scheduler'

This means future docker stack deploy calls will reliably stop old containers
within the stop_grace_period window (now 30m in stack config).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Align testfw CI with framework layout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix PE spawn deadlock, RunStartedUtc watermark, Research.md parser

- Fix _resourceRequestPending deadlock in EvaluateResourceNeedsAsync that
  prevented spawning additional PEs after the first worker was created.
  The flag now clears after SpawnCooldown so re-evaluation can occur.
- Add RunStartedUtc watermark to AgentStateStore (run_metadata table)
  to filter stale GitHub data from previous runs in dashboard views.
- Fix Research.md parser to handle code blocks and continuation lines
  instead of splitting every line into a separate bullet point.
- Filter dashboard PRs, issues, and engineering plan by RunStartedUtc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
